task_completion.description: CoppeliaSim is open and the correct scene is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CoppeliaSim application window is visible, and the scene name 'messaging/movementViaRemoteApi.ttt' is shown in the title bar or scene hierarchy.

task_completion.description: A script is created to control the robot via ZMQ Remote API and record dynamics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A code editor (e.g., VS Code, terminal editor) shows a script containing ZMQ Remote API connection (port 23000), stepping mode configuration, and logic for moving through at least 6 waypoints.

task_completion.description: The script is executed to drive the robot arm in the simulation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows the script running, and/or the CoppeliaSim simulation is in a 'play' state with the robot arm having moved from its initial position.

task_completion.description: The dynamics profile CSV file is exported with the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal shows 'dynamics_profile.csv' in '/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/'. If the file contents are visible, it must include headers for time_s, j0_pos_deg to j5_pos_deg, j0_vel_degs to j5_vel_degs, and j0_acc_degs2 to j5_acc_degs2.

task_completion.description: The compliance report JSON file is exported with the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal shows 'dynamics_report.json' in '/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/'. If the file contents are visible, it must include fields: total_samples, total_waypoints, trajectory_duration_s, max_velocity_degs, max_acceleration_degs2, velocity_violations, acceleration_violations, and compliant.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the ZMQ Remote API to interact with CoppeliaSim.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script contains genuine CoppeliaSim ZMQ Remote API calls (e.g., sim.simx... or sim...) rather than just generating random numbers or mocking the data.

integrity.description: The CSV and JSON files were generated programmatically, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's script contains file writing logic (e.g., csv.writer, json.dump) and there is no evidence of the agent manually typing the JSON or CSV contents into a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent executed the script to gather real simulation data.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is terminal output showing the execution of the Python script, rather than the agent just writing the script and skipping execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output CSV path: /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/dynamics_profile.csv. Output JSON path: /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/dynamics_report.json. Velocity limit: 180 deg/s. Acceleration limit: 800 deg/s^2. Minimum waypoints: 6. Minimum samples: 100. Minimum joint range: 45 degrees in at least 2 joints. CSV must contain columns: time_s, j0_pos_deg to j5_pos_deg, j0_vel_degs to j5_vel_degs, and j0_acc_degs2 to j5_acc_degs2. JSON must contain fields: total_samples, total_waypoints, trajectory_duration_s, max_velocity_degs, max_acceleration_degs2, velocity_violations, acceleration_violations, compliant.
